free_text: Preliminary results.
Neutron-proton elastic scattering spin-spin correlation
parameter measurements between 500 and 800 MeV. III.
Mixtures of C(SS),C(LS), C(LL), and C(NN).
LAMPF
Polarized protons on liquid deuterium with
neutrons at 0 degrees to the proton beam.
Protons are precessed to either L or S spin by a dipole
magnet and passed through a collimator.
Target polarization typically 75-80%.
Polarization reversed every few hours.
Polarized hydrogen target (HERA) consisting of a
mixture of 85% ethylamine and 15% borane ammonia.
Protons detected in SCM-105 magnetic
spectrometer consisting of scintillation counters,
scintillation couter hodoscope, positron-sensitive
multiwire proportional counter, and drift chambers.
2 scintillators were placed upstream and 25 downstream
from the analyzing magnet.
Relative beam polarimeter consists of scintillation
counters, a CH2 target, and brass absorbers.
Flux monitored by scintillators viewing the CH2 target.
Derived from measurement of mixed spin observables
C(sigma,L), C(lambda,L), C(lambda,lambda), etc.
Data rely heavily on data from previous measurements
reported in 1) Ditzler, et al., Phys.Rev. D46, 2792
(1992) and 2) Shima, et al., Phys.Rev. D47, 29 (1993).
Effects of polarized B and N in target estimated to be
negligible.
Corrected for background.
Statistical uncertainty in summed quadrature
with an estimate of the uncertainty in the background
subtraction.
Estimated systematic error from uncertainty in target
and beam polarization = 4%.
Mixed spin observables also measured at 567 MeV.
Revised beam polarization values derived.
C(NN) measured at 484 and 634 MeV only. Results given
in Table VI for other energies are from phase shift
analysis.
Data taken from table VI in article.
Approved by H. Spinka, 96/10/17.
Converted to new date formats, BIB update.
C(SS) and C(NN) uncertain because of rapid angular
dependence and possible errors in c.m. angle
C(SS) and C(NN) uncertain because of rapid angular
dependence and possible errors in c.m. angles
